Rundschreiben 24/2025
Bonn, 10.07.2025
Einführung von Zeiterfassungstabellen für alle Mitarbeitenden in
Technik und Verwaltung der Universität Bonn
Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, liebe Kolleg*innen,
wir möchten Sie darüber informieren, dass die Universität Bonn für alle Mitarbeiten-
den in Technik und Verwaltung (zentral und dezentral) vereinheitlichte Zeiterfas-
sungstabellen im Excel-Format einführen wird. Dies ist ein erster Schritt in der Vorbe-
reitung zur Einführung einer zentralen elektronischen Zeiterfassungsplattform an der
Universität Bonn zur zukünftigen digitalen Verwaltung von Arbeitszeiten und Abwe-
senheiten.
Mitarbeitende aus der zentralen und dezentralen Verwaltung, die bereits eine Zeit-
erfassungstabelle pflegen, können diese bis zum 31.12.2025 in aktueller Form weiter-
führen und wechseln spätestens ab 01.01.2026 auf die neuen Tabellen. Ein vorheriger
Wechsel auf eine neue Tabelle ist jederzeit freigestellt.
Verwaltungsmitarbeiter*innen, die noch keine Excel-Zeiterfassungstabelle nutzen,
verwenden bitte ab dem 01.09.2025 eine der jeweils bereitgestellten Zeiterfas-
sungstabellen:
• Tabelle für Mitarbeitende mit 5-Tage-Arbeitswoche
• Tabelle für Mitarbeitende mit variablen Wochenarbeitszeiten
• Tabelle für Auszubildende unter 18 Jahren
Universitätskasse Bonn:
Für die Mitarbeitenden der zentralen Universitätsverwaltung gilt dabei weiterhin die
aktuelle Dienstvereinbarung zur Flexiblen Arbeitszeit. Sparkasse KoelnBonn
BIC: COLSDE 33
Für Mitarbeitende aus den Botanischen Gärten und dem Hochschulrechenzentrum IBAN: DE08370501980000057695
(HRZ) gelten zudem weiter die Rahmenbedingungen aus den jeweiligen Dienstverein-

Circular No. 24/2025
Bonn, 7/10/2025
Introducing Time Recording Spreadsheets for all Technical and
Administrative Staff at the University of Bonn
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
The purpose of today’s circular is to inform you that the University of Bonn will be
introducing uniform Excel spreadsheets for time recording for all technical and
administrative staff (central and decentralized). This marks a first step in the
preparations for bringing in a central electronic time recording platform at the
University that will be used to manage working times and absences digitally in future.
Staff working in the central and decentralized administration who already use a time
recording spreadsheet can continue to use it until December 31, 2025. They will need
to switch to the new spreadsheets by January 1, 2026 at the latest but are welcome
to do so beforehand if they prefer.
From September 1, 2025 onward, administrative staff who do not yet use an Excel
spreadsheet for time recording should use the appropriate one for them:
• Spreadsheet for staff working a five-day week
• Spreadsheet for staff whose working hours vary week to week
• Spreadsheet for apprentices under 18
Bank account of the
University of Bonn:
The current Civil Service Staff Council Agreement on Flexible Working Time will
continue to apply to staff in Central Administration. Sparkasse KoelnBonn
BIC: COLSDE 33
In addition, Botanic Garden and University IT staff will continue to be covered by the IBAN: DE08370501980000057695
terms of their respective civil service staff council agreements. They will likewise be
VAT no.:
expected to use the new centrally supplied spreadsheets to record their working
DE 122 119 125
times from January 1, 2026 at the latest.

en_mt:
Introduction of time recording tables for all staff in technical and administrative departments at the University of Bonn

Dear colleagues,

We would like to inform you that the University of Bonn will introduce standardized time recording tables in Excel format for all staff in technical and administrative departments (central and decentralized). This is the first step in preparing for the future introduction of a central electronic time recording platform at the University of Bonn, enabling the digital management of working hours and absences.

Staff from central and decentralized administration who already maintain a time recording table may continue using their current version until December 31, 2025, and must switch to the new tables at the latest from January 1, 2026. However, switching to the new tables earlier is possible at any time.

Administrative staff who currently do not use an Excel time recording table are requested to use one of the provided time recording tables starting September 1, 2025:

• Table for staff with a 5-day workweek  
• Table for staff with variable weekly working hours  
• Table for apprentices under 18 years of age  

University Treasury Bonn:  
For staff in the central university administration, the current collective agreement on flexible working hours remains applicable.  

Sparkasse KölnBonn  
BIC: COLSDE33  
IBAN: DE08370501980000057695  
USt.-Id-Nr.: DE 122 119 125  

For staff from the Botanical Gardens and the University Computing Center (HRZ), the respective terms of the applicable collective agreements continue to apply.

BONN
barungen. Zur Erfassung der Arbeitszeiten sollen ebenfalls spätestens ab dem
01.01.2026 die neuen zentral zur Verfügung gestellten Tabellen genutzt werden.
Mitarbeitende der ULB nutzen weiterhin das bestehende Zeiterfassungssystem unter
Anwendung des aktuellen Leitfadens „Gleitzeit“.
Im Serviceportal finden Mitarbeitende über folgenden Link eine umfassende Infor-
mationsseite zur Zeiterfassung in Verwaltung und Technik. Dort können die Zeiterfas-
sungstabellen für 2025 jederzeit heruntergeladen werden. Die Zeiterfassungstabellen
2026 werden dort zu gegebener Zeit ebenfalls bereitgestellt.
Verwaltungsmitarbeiter*innen beachten bitte insbesondere die jeweiligen Leitfä-
den:
• Leitfaden Zentrale Verwaltung (auf Basis der aktuellen Dienstvereinbarung)
• Leitfaden Dezentrale Verwaltung
Die Zeiterfassungstabellen müssen der*dem jeweiligen Vorgesetzte*n digital monat-
lich vorgelegt werden. Die*der Vorgesetzte hat die Zeiterfassung zur Wahrung der
Fürsorgepflicht auf die Einhaltung der rechtlichen Vorschriften zu prüfen und zu ge-
nehmigen. Ein besonderes Augenmerk soll hierbei vor allem auf die Einhaltung von
Soll- und Höchstarbeitszeiten, Pausen und Ruhezeiten gelegt werden. Die Tabellen
sind in geeigneter Weise aufzubewahren, wobei der Zugriff auf die Dateien aus-
schließlich der*dem jeweiligen Vorgesetzten und der*dem betroffenen
Mitarbeiter*in vorbehalten sind. Die Zeiterfassungstabellen sind auf Anfrage im
begründeten Bedarfsfall der Personalabteilung und/oder der Referentin für
Zeitwirtschaft Frau Petra Schüller zur Verfügung zu stellen.
Für fachliche und technische Rückfragen nutzen Sie bitte das Serviceformular auf dem
Serviceportal. Bitte konsultieren Sie auch jederzeit die bereitgestellten Leitfäden und
die Ausfüllhilfe für die Zeiterfassungstabellen im Serviceportal unter oben genannten
Link.
Wir danken Ihnen für Ihre Unterstützung!
Mit freundlichen Grüßen
gez.
Holger Gottschalk
Kanzler

RHEINISCHE
FRIEDRICH-WILHELMS- Division 3
UNIVERSITÄT Human Resources
BONN
Bonn University and State Library staff will continue to use their existing time
recording system, applying the latest version of their flextime guidelines.
The Service Portal has a page with detailed information on time recording for
technical and administrative staff—simply click on this link. The time recording
spreadsheets from 2025 can be downloaded from this page at any time, while those
for 2026 will likewise be uploaded in due course.
In particular, administrative staff are asked to follow the guidelines relevant to
them:
• Guidelines for Central Administration (based on the current civil service staff
council agreement)
• Guidelines for Decentralized Administration
The time recording spreadsheets must be submitted electronically to the relevant line
manager every month. In order to fulfill their duty of care, the line manager is
required to check that the times recorded comply with the legal requirements and, if
so, to approve them. In particular, they must focus on making sure that the target
number of hours have been worked, that no maximum limits have been exceeded
and that breaks and rest periods have been taken as stipulated. The time recording
spreadsheets must be stored in an appropriate manner such that the files can only be
accessed by the line manager and employee concerned. The spreadsheets are to be
made available to Human Resources and/or to the Time Recording Administrative
Specialist Petra Schüller on request if there is a justified need.
Please use the service form in the Service Portal if you have any technical or other
specific queries. You are also invited at any time to consult the guidelines provided
and the tips for filling in the time recording spreadsheets, which you can find by
following the Service Portal link given above.
Thank you very much for your support!
Sincerely,
signed
Holger Gottschalk
Provost

At the end of the ULB, employees will continue to use the existing time recording system in accordance with the current "Flexitime" guidelines.

In the Service Portal, staff can find a comprehensive information page on time recording in administration and technical departments via the following link. Here, the time recording tables for 2025 can be downloaded at any time. The time recording tables for 2026 will also be made available there when appropriate.

Administrative staff are asked to pay particular attention to the respective guidelines:

• Guidelines for Central Administration (based on the current service agreement)
• Guidelines for Decentralized Administration

The time recording tables must be submitted digitally to the respective supervisor on a monthly basis. The supervisor is responsible for reviewing and approving the time recording to ensure compliance with legal regulations and to fulfill their duty of care. Special attention should be paid primarily to compliance with target and maximum working hours, breaks, and rest periods. The tables must be stored appropriately, with access to the files restricted exclusively to the respective supervisor and the affected employee. The time recording tables must be made available upon request to the Personnel Department and/or to Ms. Petra Schüller, Departmental Officer for Time Management, in justified cases of need.

For technical or subject-related questions, please use the service form on the Service Portal. Please also consult the provided guidelines and the filling aid for the time recording tables in the Service Portal via the link mentioned above at any time.

Thank you for your support!

Kind regards

signed  
Holger Gottschalk  
Chancellor

Rundschreiben 26/2025 Bonn, den 24.07.2025
Ende der (Sicherheits-) Updates von Windows 10
Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, liebe Kolleg*innen,
mit diesem Rundschreiben möchten wir Sie darüber informieren, dass ab dem 14.10.2025 ei-
nige aktuell in der Universität eingesetzten Produkte von Microsoft keine Updates mehr erhal-
ten werden. Dies gilt unter anderem für Windows 10, diverse ältere Windows 11 Varianten
und Microsoft Office 2016 und 2019. Dies führt dazu, dass neu entdeckte Sicherheitslücken
von Microsoft ab diesem Zeitpunkt nicht mehr geschlossen werden. Geräte, auf denen das je-
weilige Produkt zu dem Zeitpunkt noch im Einsatz ist, werden dadurch anfällig für Cyberan-
griffe. Der Einsatz dieser Produkte nach dem 14.10.2025 stellt daher ein nicht vertretbares Ri-
siko dar.
Im Folgenden möchten wir Ihnen Ihre Handlungsmöglichkeiten aufzeigen.
Bitte stimmen Sie sich zu diesem Thema immer mit ihren lokalen Administrator*innen ab oder
kontaktieren Sie bei weiterführenden Fragen die Digitalisierungsmanager*innen oder das HRZ.
Mitarbeiter*innen, welche ihr Gerät von Dezernat 2 erhalten haben, müssen weder selbst ak-
tiv werden, noch den Support kontaktieren.
Wenn Sie aktuell eine der oben genannten Versionen von Microsoft Windows nutzen, empfeh-
len wir eine der folgenden Möglichkeiten:
1. Update auf ein aktuelles Windows 11. Wir empfehlen den Umstieg auf die Editionen
Education oder Enterprise, da diese Editionen weitergehende Konfigurationsmöglich-
keiten im Sinne der DSGVO bieten. Nach der Installation können Sie optional, zur Kon-
figuration der Privatsphäreeinstellungen, auf das kostenlose Tool ShutUp10++ zurück-
greifen. Empfehlungen für die Konfiguration finden Sie im Serviceportal.
Bitte beachten Sie, dass nicht alle Geräte Windows 11 fähig sind. Eine Übersicht über
die nötigen Systemanforderungen Ihres Gerätes finden Sie auf der Seite von Micro-
soft.
2. Sollte Ihr Gerät nicht Windows 11 fähig sein, empfehlen wir den Austausch und Um-
stieg auf ein neueres Gerät, auf welchem Windows 11 (bevorzugt Education oder
Enterprise) bereits vorinstalliert ist oder installiert werden kann.

Circular No. 26/2025 Bonn, July 24, 2025
Support (security updates) for Windows 10 ending
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
In this circular we wish to inform you that some Microsoft products currently in use at the Uni-
versity will not be supported after October 14, 2025. Accordingly, Windows 10, older versions
of Windows 11 and Microsoft Office 2016 and 2019 will no longer receive updates. This means
that emerging security vulnerabilities will no longer be fixed by Microsoft as of that date. De-
vices running the affected products at that time will be vulnerable to cyberattack. Use of these
products after October 14, 2025 therefore represents an unacceptable risk.
Your available options for addressing this situation are outlined below.
You are requested to please coordinate with your local administrators on this issue, or contact
the Digitalization Managers or University IT if you have any further questions.
Employees who received their device from Division 2 do not have to contact Support or take
any other steps.
If you are currently using one of the above-mentioned versions of Microsoft Windows, you
have the following recommended options:
1. Update to a current version of Windows 11. We recommend switching to the Educa-
tion or Enterprise editions, as these have more GDPR-relevant configuration options.
Post-installation you can use the free, optional tool ShutUp10++ to configure privacy
settings. Configuration recommendations are posted in the Service Portal.
Please note that not all devices are able to run Windows 11. Visit the Microsoft web-
site for an overview of the system requirements which your device must meet.
2. If your device is unable to run Windows 11, we recommend switching to a more
modern device which has Windows 11 pre-installed (preferably the Education or En-
terprise editions), or is capable of running these.
3. If you are unable to switch to a new device immediately for financial reasons, or if
continuing to use Windows 10 makes sense for you for other reasons, you can use the
Pro version of the software 0patch as a temporary solution until you purchase a new
device. Microsoft extended security updates currently require a “Campus EES con-
tract”.

Wenn Sie aktuell eine der oben genannten Versionen von Microsoft Windows nutzen, empfeh-
len wir eine der folgenden Möglichkeiten:
1. Update auf ein aktuelles Windows 11. Wir empfehlen den Umstieg auf die Editionen
Education oder Enterprise, da diese Editionen weitergehende Konfigurationsmöglich-
keiten im Sinne der DSGVO bieten. Nach der Installation können Sie optional, zur Kon-
figuration der Privatsphäreeinstellungen, auf das kostenlose Tool ShutUp10++ zurück-
greifen. Empfehlungen für die Konfiguration finden Sie im Serviceportal.
Bitte beachten Sie, dass nicht alle Geräte Windows 11 fähig sind. Eine Übersicht über
die nötigen Systemanforderungen Ihres Gerätes finden Sie auf der Seite von Micro-
soft.
2. Sollte Ihr Gerät nicht Windows 11 fähig sein, empfehlen wir den Austausch und Um-
stieg auf ein neueres Gerät, auf welchem Windows 11 (bevorzugt Education oder
Enterprise) bereits vorinstalliert ist oder installiert werden kann.

Support (security updates) for Windows 10 ending
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
In this circular we wish to inform you that some Microsoft products currently in use at the Uni-
versity will not be supported after October 14, 2025. Accordingly, Windows 10, older versions
of Windows 11 and Microsoft Office 2016 and 2019 will no longer receive updates. This means
that emerging security vulnerabilities will no longer be fixed by Microsoft as of that date. De-
vices running the affected products at that time will be vulnerable to cyberattack. Use of these
products after October 14, 2025 therefore represents an unacceptable risk.
Your available options for addressing this situation are outlined below.
You are requested to please coordinate with your local administrators on this issue, or contact
the Digitalization Managers or University IT if you have any further questions.
Employees who received their device from Division 2 do not have to contact Support or take
any other steps.
If you are currently using one of the above-mentioned versions of Microsoft Windows, you
have the following recommended options:
1. Update to a current version of Windows 11. We recommend switching to the Educa-
tion or Enterprise editions, as these have more GDPR-relevant configuration options.
Post-installation you can use the free, optional tool ShutUp10++ to configure privacy
settings. Configuration recommendations are posted in the Service Portal.
Please note that not all devices are able to run Windows 11. Visit the Microsoft web-
site for an overview of the system requirements which your device must meet.
2. If your device is unable to run Windows 11, we recommend switching to a more
modern device which has Windows 11 pre-installed (preferably the Education or En-
terprise editions), or is capable of running these.
3. If you are unable to switch to a new device immediately for financial reasons, or if
continuing to use Windows 10 makes sense for you for other reasons, you can use the
Pro version of the software 0patch as a temporary solution until you purchase a new
device. Microsoft extended security updates currently require a “Campus EES con-
tract”.
en_mt:
End of (security) updates for Windows 10

Dear colleagues,

With this notice, we would like to inform you that as of October 14, 2025, some Microsoft products currently in use at the university will no longer receive updates. This includes Windows 10, various older versions of Windows 11, and Microsoft Office 2016 and 2019. As a result, any newly discovered security vulnerabilities from Microsoft will no longer be patched from that date onward. Devices still running these products at that time will become vulnerable to cyberattacks. Continuing to use these products after October 14, 2025, therefore poses an unacceptable risk.

Below, we outline your possible actions.

Please always coordinate with your local administrators on this matter, or contact the digitalization managers or the HRZ for further questions.

Staff members who received their device from Department 2 do not need to take any action themselves nor contact support.

If you currently use one of the Microsoft Windows versions mentioned above, we recommend one of the following options:

1. Upgrade to a current version of Windows 11. We recommend switching to the Education or Enterprise editions, as these offer more configuration options in line with GDPR requirements. After installation, you may optionally use the free tool ShutUp10++ to configure privacy settings. Instructions for configuration are available in the service portal.

Please note that not all devices are compatible with Windows 11. You can find an overview of your device's required system specifications on Microsoft's website.

2. If your device is not compatible with Windows 11, we recommend replacing it and switching to a newer device on which Windows 11 (preferably Education or Enterprise) is already pre-installed or can be installed.

Rundschreiben 27/2025
Bonn, den 24.07.2025
Einführung der Multifaktor-Authentifizierung
Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, liebe Kolleg*innen,
die Universität Bonn arbeitet aktiv an der Steigerung der Cyber-Sicherheit
unserer elektronischen Dienste. Wie bereits im Herbst 2024 angekündigt, führt
die Universität dazu u.a. die Multifaktor-Authentifizierung (MFA) für die
Anmeldung mit der Uni-ID ein. Die Einführung der MFA wird im Rahmen eines
DiCe-Projektes koordiniert und wurde mit den zentralen Gremien
vereinbart. Alle Mitarbeitenden der Universität werden schrittweise mit einem
Sicherheitsschlüssel in Form eines sogenannten Yubikeys als zweitem Faktor
ausgestattet. Studierende sind hiervon zunächst nicht betroffen.
Warum MFA?
Die Uni-ID gewährt Zugriff auf eine Vielzahl elektronischer Dienste, die in vielen
Fällen auch mit Zugriff auf personenbezogene Daten oder sensible Prozesse der
IT-Infrastruktur einhergehen. Eine Absicherung lediglich mit einem Passwort ist
für einige Dienste daher nicht ausreichend, da diese wiederverwendbar sind,
leicht gestohlen oder einfach entschlüsselt werden können.
Die MFA ist zudem ein wesentlicher Bestandteil, um die Vorgaben des BSI-
Grundschutzes zu erfüllen, zu dessen Einführung sich alle Hochschulen in NRW
gegenüber dem Land verpflichtet haben.

Introduction of multi-factor authentication
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
The University of Bonn works actively to heighten cybersecurity for our
electronic services. As announced in the fall of 2024, the University will be
introducing multi-factor authentication (MFA) when logging in via Uni-ID,
among other measures. The MFA rollout is being coordinated within the
f ramework of a DiCe project, and has been agreed with the central committees
and bodies. All University employees will receive a Yubikey security key as
s econd factor in a phased rollout process. Students are not affected by the
change at this time.
Why use MFA?
The Uni-ID is used as access credential for a wide range of electronic services
which in many cases involve the accessing of personal data or sensitive IT
infrastructure processes. For certain services, password-only security is
insufficient, as passwords are often reused and easily stolen and crackable.
MFA is also an important criterion for fulfilling requirements under the BSI-
Grundschutz basic security standard, which all universities in the state of North
Rhine-Westphalia have agreed to uphold. Bank details:

en_mt:
Introduction of Multi-Factor Authentication

Dear Ladies and Gentlemen, dear colleagues,

the University of Bonn is actively working to enhance the cybersecurity of our electronic services. As previously announced in autumn 2024, the university will introduce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for logging in with the Uni-ID. The rollout of MFA is being coordinated as part of a DiCe project and has been agreed upon with the central university bodies. All university staff will gradually be provided with a security key in the form of a so-called YubiKey as a second authentication factor. Students are not affected at this stage.

Why MFA?

The Uni-ID provides access to a wide range of electronic services, many of which involve access to personal data or sensitive processes within the IT infrastructure. Relying solely on a password is therefore insufficient for some services, as passwords can be reused, easily stolen, or simply decrypted.

MFA is also an essential component for meeting the requirements of the BSI-Grundschutz, which all universities in North Rhine-Westphalia have committed to implementing in cooperation with the state.

RHEINISCHE
FRIEDRICH-WILHELMS-
UNIVERSITÄT BONN
Seite 2
Die Nutzung der Multifaktor-Authentifizierung wird zunächst für die
Anmeldung an folgenden Diensten eingeführt:
• SAP
• Confluence
• Identity-Management-System GOsa
• Mailinglisten
Perspektivisch wird der Anmeldevorgang weiterer elektronischer Dienste durch
MFA abgesichert werden.
Zeitplan:
• Der Rollout und die Verteilung der Sicherheitsschlüssel erfolgen
schrittweise. Sie erhalten eine persönliche Mail an Ihre Uni-Bonn-
Mailadresse, sobald Ihre Organisationseinheit an der Reihe ist.
• Solange Sie noch keine Mail erhalten haben, können Sie sich weiterhin
wie gewohnt bei den oben genannten Diensten anmelden.
• Die lokalen IT-Admins der Fachbereiche, die Universitätsverwaltung und
die Mitarbeitenden des HRZ wurden bereits mit den
Yubikeys ausgestattet.
• Wir haben mit dem Rollout an der Philosophischen Fakultät und der
ersten TRA-Einheit begonnen.
• Voraussichtlich bis zum Ende des Jahres 2025 wird der Rollout in allen
Einrichtungen der Universität gestartet sein.
Anleitungen und Support
Hilfe erhalten Sie über die HRZ-Doku auf Confluence
(https://confluence.team.uni-bonn.de/x/oT4rD) und über den MFA-Support des
HRZ via Mail an mfa-support@uni-bonn.de. Dort erhalten Sie Unterstützung zu
allen Aspekten der Multifaktor-Authentifizierung für die Uni-ID. Auch für den
Fall des Verlusts oder Defekts eines Yubikeys, auch z.B. auf Dienstreisen, finden
Sie dort die Anleitungen und Supportprozesse, die sicherstellen, dass Sie in
diesem Falle möglichst schnell wieder arbeitsfähig sind.
Was müssen Sie tun?
Für Mitarbeitende ist die Nutzung von Sicherheitsschlüsseln (‚Yubikeys‘)
obligatorisch. Als Schutzmaßnahme gegen Phishing-Angriffe und zur Sicherung
sensibler Daten wurde ein Hardwaresicherheitsschlüssel gewählt, da er durch
en_ref:
RHEINISCHE
FRIEDRICH-WILHELMS-
UNIVERSITÄT BONN
Page 2
Multi-factor authentication will initially be introduced for logging into the
following services:
• SAP
• Confluence
• Gosa identity management system
• Mailing lists
At a later point, MFA security will be rolled out for the login process to other
electronic services.
Timeline:
• Security keys are to be rolled out and distributed in phases. When your
organizational unit is ready for rollout, you will receive a personalized
email in your University of Bonn email address.
• You can continue to log in to the services as usual until you not receive
the email.
• Local departmental IT administrators, central administration and
University IT staff have already been given Yubikeys.
• The rollout commenced with the Faculty of Arts and the first TRA unit.
• The rollout is expected to be underway University-wide by the end of
2025.
Instructions and support
For help see the University IT documentation on Confluence
(https://confluence.team.uni-bonn.de/x/oT4rD) or contact the University IT
MFA support desk by email at mfa-support@uni-bonn.de for assistance on any
issues concerning multi-factor authentication for Uni-ID login. The available
instructions and support will ensure that you can get back to work as quickly as
possible after encountering any problems, such as a lost or broken Yubikey
occurring on a business trip.
What do you have to do?
The use of security keys (‘Yubikeys’) is mandatory for all employees. A hardware
security key was chosen because it offers greater security than software-based
solutions, due to being physically separate from the user device. These keys
protect against phishing attacks and allow secure storage of sensitive data.

RHEINISCHE
FRIEDRICH-WILHELMS-
UNIVERSITÄT BONN
Seite 3
die physische Trennung vom Endgerät besonders widerstandsfähig ist und eine
höhere Sicherheit gegenüber softwarebasierten Lösungen bietet.
Je nach Organisationseinheit erhalten Sie Ihren persönlichen Yubikey entweder
direkt vom HRZ oder ihrer lokalen Ausgabestelle. Die Information, welche
Ausgabestelle für Sie zuständig ist und in welchem Zeitraum der Yubikey für
Sie bereitliegt, erhalten Sie per Mail an ihre Uni-Bonn-Mailadresse.
• Dieser Schlüssel kann an allen Endgeräten eingesetzt werden.
• Wir empfehlen, ihn am Dienstschlüsselbund zu tragen, damit Sie ihn
immer bei sich führen. Bitte lassen Sie Ihren Yubikey nicht
unbeaufsichtigt liegen.
• Wenn Ihr Dienstverhältnis an der Universität Bonn endet, müssen Sie
den Yubikey an die Ausgabestelle zurückgeben.
Uns ist bewusst, dass die Multifaktor-Authentifizierung für Sie zunächst einen
zusätzlichen Arbeitsschritt darstellt. Sie ist aber einer der wichtigsten
Bestandteile zur Stärkung der Cybersicherheit an der Universität Bonn. Wir
minimieren damit das Risiko, durch einen Cyberangriff weitreichende und
langdauernde Systemausfälle und / oder Datenverluste zu erleiden. Die
bisherigen Erfahrungen aus dem Rollout der Multifaktor-Authentifizierung
mittels Yubikey in der zentralen Universitätsverwaltung zeigen zudem, dass die
Authentifizierung de facto nur einen minimalen Zeitaufwand bedeutet.
Mit freundlichen Grüßen

Depending on your organizational unit, you will receive your personal Yubikey
either from University IT directly or from a local distribution point. You will be
informed by email to your University of Bonn email address of your
distribution point and when your Yubikey is available for pickup.
• The key can be used on any user device.
• We recommend carrying it on your work keychain so you have it with
you at all times. Please make sure to not leave your Yubikey anywhere
unattended.
• Upon ending employment at the University of Bonn, the Yubikey must
be returned to the distribution point.
We are aware that multi-factor authentication represents an additional step
that you are not used to. It is however one of the most important means of
achieving more robust cybersecurity for the University of Bonn. This measure
minimizes risk of a cyberattack resulting in system failures and/or data losses,
with potentially far-reaching and long-lasting consequences. Yubikeys have
already been rolled out to central administration for multi-factor
authentication, where staff have reported that this new authentication method
really only takes a minimal amount of extra time.
Sincerely,

große Anzahl an Gastwissenschaftler*innen aus Deutschland und aller Welt empfangen

nur Beschäftigten der Universität Bonn zur Verfügung stehen.

Regelungen als für Mitglieder der Universität. Aus diesem Grund und auch aus arbeits-
schutzrechtlichen Gründen ist es wichtig, ihren Aufenthalt an der Universität Bonn im
Rahmen von schriftlichen Vereinbarungen zu regeln.

Bonn, 28/07/2025
Dear Members of the University,
Expanding the University of Bonn’s research and teaching network lies at the heart of
its strategy and success. We are therefore delighted to be able to welcome so many
guest researchers from Germany and around the world to our University every year.
Following invitations from University of Bonn researchers, guest researchers come and
spend time carrying out research at our institution. While they have no formal connec-
tion to the University, guest researchers gain access to resources such as data, labora-
tories and equipment that are usually only available to University of Bonn staff.
Different regulations apply to guest researchers than to members of the University in
terms of accident and health insurance. It is therefore important to draw up written
agreements regarding their time at the University of Bonn, not least for occupational
health and safety reasons.
Relevant information was already shared in Circular No. 31/2023 “Occupational Health
and Safety for Visitors.” Since then, existing processes around the registration of guest
researchers have been optimized and digitalized.

Introduction of a mandatory digital process for onboarding visiting researchers  
Bonn, 28.07.2025  

Dear members of the university,  
The networking of the University of Bonn as an Excellence University in research and teaching is central to its strategy and success. Therefore, we are extremely pleased to welcome a large number of visiting researchers from Germany and around the world each year. Visiting researchers are individuals without formal affiliation to the university who are hosted on university premises and in university laboratories at the invitation of university researchers for the purpose of research. They have access to resources such as data, laboratory spaces, and equipment that are otherwise only available to university employees.

Different regulations apply to these individuals regarding accident and insurance protection compared to university members. For this reason, and also due to occupational safety regulations, it is important to regulate their stay at the University of Bonn through written agreements.

We previously informed you about this in circular letter No. 31/2023, "Occupational Safety for External Persons." Since then, we have optimized and digitized the existing processes related to onboarding visiting researchers.

University Treasury Bonn:  
If you would like to invite visiting researchers, we kindly ask you from now on to register them exclusively through this form as early as possible before their planned stay. After registration, an internal administrative review process begins, at the end of which your visiting researchers will typically receive a corresponding contractual agreement, a hosting agreement for visa applications, VAT identification number: DE 122 119 125.

Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, liebe Kolleg*innen,
hiermit informieren wir Sie über wichtige Fristen für den Jahresabschluss im Personal-
management:
Alle Personalmaßnahmen,
• die zum 1. bzw. zum 15. Januar 2026 beginnen sollen, sind bis spätestens
Freitag, 24. Oktober 2025 bzw.
• die zum 1. Februar 2026 beginnen sollen, sind bis spätestens
Dienstag, 4. November 2025
vollständig in Ihrer zuständigen Personalabteilung einzureichen.
Hintergrund ist, dass wir den reibungslosen Prozessablauf aller Personalmaßnahmen
rechtzeitig und in sehr guter Qualität auch über den Jahresabschluss hinaus sicherstel-
len wollen.
In diesem Zusammenhang möchten wir auf die Bearbeitungsdauer von Anträgen jegli-
cher Art (Personalmaßnahmen, Lehrbeauftragte, etc.) von sechs Wochen erinnern, die
sich ggfs. durch erforderliche Prüfungen oder fehlende Unterlagen auch verlängern
kann. Neben der sorgfältigen Bearbeitung Ihrer Anträge und Erstellung der erforderli-
chen vertraglichen Unterlagen stellen wir auch die ordnungsgemäße Beteilung der Gre-
mien sicher. Gerne können Sie uns deshalb auch bereits jetzt Vorgänge für das Jahr
2026 einreichen.
Wir danken Ihnen für Ihr Verständnis und die kollegiale Zusammenarbeit. Bei weiteren
Fragen können Sie sich jederzeit an uns wenden.
Mit freundlichen Grüßen

Circular No. 29/2025 File ref. 3.2
Key Deadlines for Year-End Closing in HR for 2025/2026
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
This circular is intended to give you information about some important deadlines for
year-end closing in HR:
All staffing measures that are to begin on
• January 1 or 15, 2026 must be submitted in full to your HR section by
Friday, October 24, 2025 at the latest.
• February 1, 2026 must be submitted in full to your HR section by
Tuesday, November 4, 2025 at the latest.
This is so that we can ensure in good time that all HR processes continue to run
smoothly and in very high quality, including over the year-end closing period.
We would like to point out in this regard that requests of any kind (staffing measures,
contract lecturers, etc.) take six weeks to process, or potentially even longer if any
checks are needed or documents are missing. As well as processing your requests with
due care and preparing the necessary contractual documentation, we will also ensure
that the various bodies are involved as appropriate. You are therefore welcome to sub-
mit requests for 2026 even at this early stage.
Thank you for your understanding and good teamwork. Please do not hesitate to con-
tact us at any time if you have any questions.
Sincerely,
signed Thomas Rosenkranz
Human Resources Director Bank account

All staffing measures that are to begin on
• January 1 or 15, 2026 must be submitted in full to your HR section by
Friday, October 24, 2025 at the latest.
• February 1, 2026 must be submitted in full to your HR section by
Tuesday, November 4, 2025 at the latest.
This is so that we can ensure in good time that all HR processes continue to run
smoothly and in very high quality, including over the year-end closing period.
We would like to point out in this regard that requests of any kind (staffing measures,
contract lecturers, etc.) take six weeks to process, or potentially even longer if any
checks are needed or documents are missing. As well as processing your requests with
due care and preparing the necessary contractual documentation, we will also ensure
that the various bodies are involved as appropriate. You are therefore welcome to sub-
mit requests for 2026 even at this early stage.
Thank you for your understanding and good teamwork. Please do not hesitate to con-
tact us at any time if you have any questions.
Sincerely,
signed Thomas Rosenkranz
Human Resources Director Bank account
Sparkasse KoelnBonn

IBAN: DE08370501980000057695
en_mt:
Important deadlines for the 2025/2026 personnel year-end closing

Dear Ladies and Gentlemen, dear colleagues,

We inform you about important deadlines for the year-end closing in personnel management:

All personnel actions:
• those intended to begin on 1 January or 15 January 2026 must be submitted in full to your responsible personnel department by Friday, 24 October 2025, and
• those intended to begin on 1 February 2026 must be submitted in full by Tuesday, 4 November 2025.

The reason for these deadlines is that we aim to ensure the smooth and timely processing of all personnel actions, maintaining high quality standards even beyond the year-end closing period.

In this context, we would like to remind you of the processing period of six weeks for applications of any kind (personnel actions, visiting lecturers, etc.), which may be extended if necessary due to required reviews or missing documents. In addition to carefully processing your applications and preparing the required contractual documents, we also ensure proper involvement of the relevant committees. Therefore, please feel free to submit procedures for 2026 with us as early as now.

Thank you for your understanding and collegial cooperation. Should you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Kind regards,

On behalf of  
Bank details  
Signed: Thomas Rosenkranz  
Personnel Division Head Sparkasse KoelnBonn

Circular No. 30/2025
Closure times of Central Administration and regulations applicable
to working time between Christmas and the end of 2025/start of
2026
Dear Sir or Madam, dear colleagues,
This circular informs you about the regulations applicable to working time at the
University of Bonn’s Central Administration between Christmas and the end of
2025/start of 2026.
Central Administration will remain closed between December 24, 2025 and January 1,
2026 inclusive.
As announced in Circular No. 26/1996, December 24 and December 31 count as days
off.
Administrative staff are free to decide whether to take flextime or holidays on
December 29 and 30, 2025. Staff who do not have sufficient holidays or flextime left for
the year can go into overdraft with their flextime accounts as necessary. The overdraft
limit of 20 hours as per Civil Service Staff Council Agreement on Flexible Working Time
in Central Administration at the University of Bonn can be exceeded accordingly.
The former Deutscher Herold building (Poppelsdorfer Allee 31–33), the University Main
Building and the AVZ III (Römerstraße 164) will be closed on those days. Any keys that
are required during this period can be obtained from Section 4.1—Infrastructure and
Facility Services (Dieter Roßbach, phone: +49 228 73-7656 and Andre Mohr, phone:
+49 228 73-4825). The remaining buildings will not be serviced by the janitors either
apart from those required for the winter road/path clearance and on-call services.
Bank account details
The winter road/path clearance and on-call services will continue to be provided during
Sparkasse KoelnBonn
this period.
BIC: COLSDE 33
IBAN: DE083705019800000576
Section 4.3—Technical Services will be on call around the clock for technical
emergencies between 3:30 pm on December 23, 2025 and 8 am on January 2, 2026. You
can call them on +49 228 73-1111.

en_mt:
Closure periods of the administration and working time regulations for the year change 2025/2026

Dear Ladies and Gentlemen, dear colleagues,

The following arrangements have been made by the administration of the University of Bonn for the year change 2025/2026:

The administration will be closed from December 24, 2025, to January 1, 2026.

Please note that December 24 and December 31 are non-working days, as previously announced in circular 26/1996.

For the period from December 29, 2025, to December 30, 2025, administrative staff are entitled to take vacation or flexitime to compensate for these days. If sufficient vacation and/or flexitime is not available, the flexitime account may be debited accordingly. The time credit limit of 20 hours specified in the service agreement on flexible working hours in the central administration of the University of Bonn may accordingly be exceeded.

The building of the former German Herold (Poppelsdorfer Allee 31–33), the main university building, and AVZ III (Römerstraße 164) will be closed on these days. If keys are required during this period, they can be collected from department 4.1 – Central Service Tasks (Mr. Roßbach, phone 0228 / 73-7656, and Mr. Mohr, phone 0228 / 73-4825). The remaining buildings will not be attended by the caretakers except for winter services and on-call duties.

Winter services and on-call duties will continue to be provided during this period.

Bank details

From December 23, 2025, 3:30 PM to January 2, 2026, 8:00 AM, the Sparkasse KoelnBonn will be available:

BIC: COLSDE 33  
IBAN: DE083705019800000576

Department 4.3 – Technical Services provides 24-hour on-call support for urgent technical emergencies. You can reach this service at phone number 0228 / 73-1111.

en_mt:
I would like to point out that December 24th and December 31st, as already announced in circular 26/1996, are non-working days.  
I kindly ask you to act accordingly in your area and to make the necessary announcements.  
The lecture-free period runs from December 24, 2025, to January 6, 2026.  
Kind regards  
signed  
Holger Gottschalk  
Chancellor
